title: Sam Altman’s OpenAI proposes handing Trump administration 5pc stake
description: The artificial intelligence start-up is in early talks for a public ownership deal as political pressure rises in the US.
content:
San Francisco | OpenAI has discussed giving a 5 per cent stake to the US government as the $US852 billion ($1.2 trillion) AI start-up seeks to clear political obstacles by securing financial buy-in from the Trump administration.

title: How Microsoft’s AI strength is fueling its stock rally
description: Microsoft shares climbed sharply as investors renewed their interest in large-cap technology companies. The rally was driven by growing confidence in artificial intelligence (AI), resilient demand for enterprise software, and optimism surrounding Microsoft's cloud business. Improving market sentiment also supported gains across the broader technology sector, with Microsoft emerging as one of the biggest beneficiaries. (Sources: Yahoo Finance, Barron's) ​Microsoft Gains as Investors Return to AI Leaders

title: Malaysia poised to gain from AI chip upcycle
description: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia is well-positioned to benefit from the global artificial intelligence (AI)-driven semiconductor upcycle through advanced packaging, AI infrastructure, semiconductor equipment demand and ongoing supply-chain diversification, said Apex Securities Bhd.

title: AI Meets Aspiration: Hindustan Campus Launches One of North India's Elite AI Experience Centers
description: Mathura (Uttar Pradesh) [India], July 2: Conceived by Garav A. Sonbhadra, the AI Experience Center at Hindustan Campus, Mathura, aims to redefine how students perceive their future by combining artificial intelligence with career inspiration. Developed around the philosophy 'Passion Hai To Possible Hai,' the initiative enables students to create AI-powered digital avatars that showcase what they could become through dedication, continuous learning, and perseverance.

title: Asian stocks mostly decline on a sell-off of chip shares
description: Asian shares are mostly lower on heavy selling of computer chip stocks, tracking losses on Wall Street. South Korea's Kospi index sank 5.1%, while Japan's Nikkei 225 lost 1.5%. Surging demand for artificial intelligence has pushed prices of related tech stocks higher, with markets in South Korea and Japan among those reaping huge gains. Oil prices slipped after negotiators from the U.S. and Iran met separately with mediators from Qatar and Pakistan. U.S. futures edged higher. On Wednesday, Wall Street's benchmark S&P 500 fell 0.2% and the Dow Jones Industrial Average slipped less than 0.1%. The tech-heavy Nasdaq composite dropped 0.7%. The heaviest weights were tech stocks, such as Micron Technology, which fell 10.6%.
content:
HONG KONG (AP) — Asian shares mostly declined Thursday on heavy selling of computer chip stocks, while U.S. futures were little changed after modest losses on Wall Street.

title: AI’s Efficiency Promise Is Colliding With Job Loss, Tax Risk and Private Debt Opacity
description: Fitch Ratings has identified artificial intelligence investment, digital infrastructure spending and private credit expansion as emerging areas of global credit risk, even as AI promises efficiency gains. The warning matters because investors are increasingly focused not only on immediate market shocks, but also on second-order effects such as job displacement, weaker tax bases, opaque lending structures and geopolitical volatility.
content: Artificial intelligence is becoming a test of capital discipline, labour-market resilience and sovereign revenue strength for global markets. Fitch Ratings has identified AI, large-scale digital infrastructure spending and the expansion of private cr... [6070 chars]
